title: Level 198
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:ㅤ}}{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-body-background-image: url(https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/0/08/Hpp.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241220095148); }}
'''Level 198'''
 {{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=5px|color=white|width=30%}}
'''Tainted Brutalism Beyond Freedom'''
 {{User:WindXD/Hr}} {{Quote|It felt empty. I guess it’s been like that for a long time. Nothing was abnormal at this level, but for once, I felt other emotions in a place designed to be emotionally detached. I feel introspective looking out the window. Nostalgia feels vague and unresolved as I gaze at the blank canvases. There are few to no hazards, nor any noise. For once, it felt like I’d just entered the Backrooms. I don’t feel welcomed, or unwelcomed. I feel like I’m in my rightful place; a wanderer destined to wander. There should be no one else breaking that pattern.|John Davis’s written report archived by [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]] in 2002.}} {{User:WindXD/Hr}} {{Alt Class|ext=2|env=2|ent=1|no=198}}
Physical Appearance
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=2px|color=white|style=dashed|width=80%}} '''Level 198''' resembles an enclosed grey concrete megastructure spanning 100x100x100km, presumably minimalistic and brutalistic in style, the style being accented in every room. Layout-wise, '''Level 198''' features short, curved corridors that lead to other hallways and interconnected rooms. The level comprises a variety of rooms named after a metallic sign present in them. Listed in order of how rare it is: viewing room (present in Fig 1), scenery view, continuity area, empty canvas, lobby, loading zone, and hotel mock. {{Blurimage|file=Hpp.jpg|caption=Introspective walk for our subject. He wonders how life was in [[the Frontrooms]].}}The viewing room is the most common room and is a small box (5x5 meters), one of the walls containing an unbreakable orange-tinted window that displays scenery of unpopulated modern cities, forest views, or nature-styled sport-intended arenas (as shown in Fig 1), always in daytime. The room contains little furniture, excluding a low, cushioned bench, a living room lamp, and, uncommonly, a rubbish bin. The rubbish bin contains full energy drink bottles or a dilated version of [[Object_27|Mixologist's Delight]]. These rooms occupy all the edges of the megastructure, though they are still common throughout the level. Notably, the windows show scenery even when they face a direction that is completely blocked out. The scenery view is generally accessible via other rooms or interconnecting hallways. The room contains a long 30-meter stretch showcasing mockups of 3D visual art, presenting statistically "pleasant" environments from the Backrooms, the next level one will enter after exiting this level, [[The Frontrooms]], the places the wanderer remembers most, and the [[The Frontrooms|Frontroom's]] resting area, all protected by a window. Unlike most rooms, the windows are breakable. However, the window regenerates every 2 hours, and if one is trapped in it during regeneration, breaking the window would be unfeasible. The continuity area is a room characterized by multiple long hallways converging into a single space. Then the appearance will shift into a glass tunnel with cliff scenery below. The room has benches and vending machines containing edible food or liquids, in which, if the wanderer uses any type of currency, it will dispense a random item. One cannot break the vending machine's glass, but the coin compartment is breakable; however, it mostly contains empty [[Object 1|Almond Water]] bottles. As with most of the other rooms, one cannot break the glass; if one tries, it will look broken, and then, when it appears about to shatter, it returns to its original form. An empty canvas is a large (50x50x50), mostly empty room with random objects or design shifts. These objects range from artworks containing extremely vague and confusing content that have been shown to cause headaches to those who overthink it, presumably via anomalous means. Other objects include: an oversized green plant in a small pot, a mixed-shape beaker, a benign liquid that tastes like [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and a telescope. Notably, those who overthink whether these objects have a purpose start to hallucinate that they do. The design shifts are characterized by slight color changes, the removal of edge curves, or the use of tiled floors. {{Quote|I mean, I hadn’t met anyone in years. Only on this level did I meet a stranger who was confused. He said he was from [[Entity Research Committee]], an organization dedicated to entities. I was supposed to be joyful meeting someone. But I felt empty. I was only drawn to my only purpose: wandering. The blank, clean grey walls were what drew me. Something lifeless compared to something alive. I questioned myself, begging to not fall back on something I call “the main nine” emotions: isolation, or dullness. And then I lost him.|Bambi Orcday’s spoken report in 2009 archived by [[B.W.O.]]}} {{Blurimage|file=Wes.jpg|caption=A picture he took of a scenic view. General features make him miss so dearly the constant loneliness he feels in the Backrooms, despite being alone.|align=left}} The lobby is the most modern-styled room, featuring a cylinder in the middle and curved concrete seats that indent the walls. They can also include a structure resembling a desk. This room typically serves as a hub for other neighboring rooms. Additionally, this is the only area affected by [[Phenomenon 15|Alone]] and exhibiting non-Euclidean features. The lobby features minor details, including clocks, a calendar, and a white neon light lining the walls. A loading zone is a moderate-sized (15x15x15 meter) room containing a garage door and a concrete statue resembling a car in the middle of the room. Notably, one can sometimes find a non-tinted window depicting scenery of any main nine-level, a movable grey metal table with drawers that may contain vitamin pills, the diluted [[Object 27|Mixologist's Delight]], a pen, notebook, or a container full of clean water; the resources are restocked every month. One must beware that the garage door may occasionally lock itself, locking a wanderer in the room for 1 to 2 hours. The hotel mock is the rarest room type, resembling a small hotel room and containing numerous resources and furniture. The room contains a queen bed, a drawer containing large full [[Object 1|Almond Water]] bottles, fruits, a map of 500 meters labeling each room, a picture of the level the wanderer was on last, a lamp from the viewing room, and a window showcasing the room you were in last.
Aberrant Detections
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=2px|color=white|style=dashed|width=80%}} '''Level 198''' is notably sterile; there is little to no bacteria. Seemingly evident when dirty liquids like [DATA LOST] and moldy [[Object 1|Almond Water]] are brought into the level, either disappearing or becoming anomalously diluted/purified into water. Bacteria cultures on the hallways, empty canvases, and beds in hotel mock-ups have led to little bacterial growth, which then dies off within a week. Although bacteria in wanderers do not disappear or exit the body, they presumably become dormant. For any ongoing major infections, they die off almost instantaneously. Measurements of the barometric pressure have led to constantly varying results, ranging between 1000 hPa and 990 hPa, low enough to cause noticeable symptoms in most individuals, and even more in those who have migraine disorders and asthma. In normal individuals, headaches/migraines, joint pain/stiffness, fatigue/weakness, dizziness, nausea, discomfort, mental fog, sinus pressure, and vertigo. People with pre-existing disorders could experience vomiting, sensitivity to light and noises, S.O.B,Shortness of breath in unabbreviated form. wheezing, and coughing fits. There are many other symptoms for people with disorders, and this has briefly mentioned the primary symptoms. The speed of sound is inconsistent at this level, varying randomly (40 to 343 meters per second) in most areas. Additionally, it varies gradually. These inconsistencies lead to slowed-down sounds, louder sounds/echoes, or no sounds present at all. Notably, the walls have proven to be highly reflective at times, with reported cases of them absorbing sound and spewing it back into the opposite hallway/room. Despite the loud echoes, the sound is compressed; the loudest noises exceeding 100 dB either become silent or change into another sound altogether. The quieter noises, 30db and lower, are boosted to be louder. If one destroys the glass of a light source,Excluding objects brought in by wanderers, the wire will start to grow quickly, 50cm every hour. These wires can also branch off in different directions, becoming thicker if they reach a dead end, and can find the closest exit to the room. The closer the wire is to the original light source, the higher the voltage it carries. The wire can only grow for 10 days before resetting back into its original form. One cannot cut the wire, as it will grow back instantly and only become thicker from then on. Experimental microscopic observation of the wires shows that the wires are continuously destroying most of their structure from the inside and reproducing it at the empty end. Windows displaying rainy weather cause a continuous temperature drop to 0 Celsius, by 1 Celsius every 15 minutes; once it drops to 0 Celsius, it will slowly rise back up to 28 Celsius, a normal level. This anomaly will affect a 50-meter radius. However, no liquids have been observed to freeze, nor have any circuits been observed to grow. They do not lose their voltage efficiency. In these rooms, the sterile effect of the level disappears in the coldest parts of the anomaly, and it has been shown to grow significantly faster than normal bacterial growth. One must avoid looking at the same window as another wanderer; if so, the scenery the wanderers see becomes mixed, creating a scene described as "vaguely confusing" that can cause headaches, brain fog, and discomfort, an effect similar to artworks spawning on an empty canvas. One will try to decipher the objects in the artwork and describe them clearly, and end up obsessing over them. After a few hours, one will start to feel guilty for quoting "wasting time" and feel compelled to look away. If one is aware of why they are currently thinking about this artwork, try to explain why it is okay not to know everything about oneself and the other wanderer. If the wanderer plans to look away abruptly somehow, they will feel an urge for weeks to come back to that window.
Lifeforms
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=2px|color=white|style=dashed|width=80%}} There have been numerous unconfirmed reports of 2 entities, both informally dubbed "CON001" and "CON002". Questioning wanderers who spread the term has shown that they can not physically name entities other than the aforementioned. There have been inconsistent reports of the appearance of both of these entities, with only a few similarities across reports: color, noise production, and shape. However, non-entity lifeforms have been observed throughout the level, typically inhabiting the scenery in the scenery view. Varying from geckos, grasshoppers, and anoles. Reports have shown that these creatures do not reproduce but spawn randomly and disappear randomly. "CON001" is an entity frequently reported when one is experiencing severe symptoms from low barometric pressure, appearing in some form as a dark blue, multi-shaped, constantly moving being, imitating unknown distorted noises. Additionally, the entity appears abruptly and is visible for up to 7 seconds. Attempts to touch CON001 result in the entity disappearing, and the symptoms of low pressure will start to appear for the next 3 levels the wanderer is in. There have been rare reports of people encountering the entity with mild symptoms. As consistently reported, however, the presence of the entity resulted in hallucinations and overthinking, lasting for 5 minutes until the symptoms abruptly disappeared. "CON002" is typically reported when one is looking at [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] or rainy scenery, resembling a completely black monolith or a humanoid completely stationary. No matter what, as soon as one notices the entity, they will start to question it. Once they start to hyper-fixate on it, the entity will disappear in the next blink. The wanderer will be compelled to leave. Now, they will encounter the entity throughout rooms for split seconds, some individuals reporting to see text floating above the entity that most of the time displays incomprehensible sentences, but also sentences described as "ominous", like "Continuous life is when one dies on the outside for the last time," and some report the sentences matching their current fears or desires.
Current Inhabitations
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=2px|color=white|style=dashed|width=80%}} A multitude of major bases have been encountered at the level, with long-term stays being doable due to common resources. Due to the minimalistic, similar nature to previous levels, no notable issues have been observed from the listed groups. The groups have proven to be passive; the lack of firearms or official melee weapons from 2 of the groups has shown so. However, they typically keep to themselves, not accepting any new members or inhabitants, considering their lack of protection.
The Continuity
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=5px|color=white|width=25%}} The Continuity is one of the first major groups on the level, presumably consisting of 260+ members to date. Forming in 2005 with only 3 members, the group grew exponentially after 2012, due to being one of the longest-lasting groups and not suffering a disappearance.It should be noted that during this time, levels between 100-200 were starting to change or suddenly disappear, killing wanderers. The Continuity sustains its base in a double continuity room, the group being made up of explorers, making a digital map of the level, copying it on paper, and sticking it on the walls close to their base. In addition to making arrows leading back to their base, and labeling certain areas. Technicians are responsible for keeping the group's website up, inserting information from the article, guiding new wanderers who just noclipped in [[Level 198]], posting new content, and managing the group's entertainment. Lastly, only 10 engineers were present in the group. They have varied purposes, though they build new makeshift printers and construct walls for the base.
Middle Contesra
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=5px|color=white|width=25%}} Middle Contesra is a medium-sized group of 131 members residing in numerous rooms and containing the most resources. The group formed in 2016, right after [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], with 10 members who met up randomly. After a few years, the group offered new wanderers food and water in exchange for their skills, leading many to join. Due to resource distribution, the leader closed the group to any new members, simply offering them food and escorting them out. The group consists of roles such as scientists, decorators, storage managers, and Scouters, initially taught by people with high skills in those roles. The group has marked the spaces most likely to be unsafe, built an [[Object 1|Almond Water]] tank, and sent new findings to The Continuity.
Entrances and Exits
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=2px|color=white|style=dashed|width=80%}}
Entrances
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=5px|color=white|width=25%}} *Entering monochrome or minimalistic thin corridors in [[Level 33]], [[Level 193]], and [[Level 197]] will lead here. *Finding floating windows in [[Level 514]] and entering them will lead here. *Noclipping in lower-lying zones in [[Level 169]] leads one here. *Entering the glass doors with signs that say "The Continuity Walk" and "Lobby Movements" in [[Level 192]] will lead to the referenced room.
Exits
{{User:WindXD/Hr|thickness=5px|color=white|width=25%}} *Finding wide corridors and no passageways to rooms for a while, you are currently in a transition point to [[Level 199]]. *If the hallways become extremely curvy and some broken parts of the hallway are on the floor, you are currently in a transition point to [[Level 200]]. *Holes in the floor send you [[Level 959|somewhere you don't want to know]].
